message:
feat(allowlist): `dcg allowlist prune` + opt-in auto-prune of expired entries

Adds an explicit way to remove expired allowlist entries from the
project / user / system layers, plus a config knob that auto-prunes
on every `dcg allowlist *` invocation for users who would rather not
keep audit history of expired entries.

CLI surface (src/cli.rs):
- New `dcg allowlist prune` subcommand under `AllowlistAction::Prune`
  with `--project` / `--user` (mutually exclusive scope flags),
  `--dry-run` (preview only — never writes), and `--format` (pretty,
  json, etc., reusing the existing `AllowlistOutputFormat`).
- New `allowlist_prune` rendering path: prints either "No expired
  allowlist entries found." or a per-entry list with selector kind /
  value / layer / reason / expires_at / ttl / source file path. JSON
  format goes through the same `AllowlistOutputFormat` machinery the
  other allowlist subcommands use, so agents get a stable shape.
- New `prune_allowlist_layers(project_only, user_only, dry_run)`
  helper that walks the relevant `AllowlistLayer` files, filters
  `AllowEntry` rows through the new `is_expiration_expired` helper,
  rewrites the file when not in dry-run, and returns the
  `Vec<PrunedAllowlistEntry>` for both the new subcommand and the
  auto-prune path.
- `handle_allowlist_command` now takes the new `auto_prune_expired:
  bool` argument; when set and the user is not already running
  `Prune`, we silently prune in-place before dispatching the actual
  subcommand. The `Prune` subcommand itself does not auto-prune to
  avoid double-rewriting the file.

Config (src/config.rs):
- New `AllowlistConfig` struct with `auto_prune_expired: bool`
  (default false — preserve audit history) and matching
  `AllowlistConfigLayer` for layered TOML merging.
- Wired into the config struct, the layer merge in
  `Config::merge_layer`, the default constructor, and the example TOML
  template emitted by `Config::default_toml`.
- Two test cases: defaults stay false, and a TOML with
  `[allowlist]` `auto_prune_expired = true` parses correctly.

Allowlist (src/allowlist.rs):
- Extract the timestamp/TTL portion of `is_expired(&AllowEntry)` into
  a public free helper `is_expiration_expired(expires_at, ttl,
  added_at)` that takes the three relevant fields as `Option<&str>`.
  This lets the prune path test rows (which it has loaded into
  intermediate structs while rewriting the TOML) without having to
  reconstruct a fully-populated `AllowEntry`. `is_expired` now
  forwards to it, so its existing semantics — including the comment
  about session-scoped validity living in `session_scope_matches` —
  are preserved.

README.md:
- Document the new `dcg allowlist prune` and `dcg allowlist prune
  --dry-run` examples next to the existing `dcg allowlist validate`
  block.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2107,6 +2107,16 @@ dcg allowlist validate
 dcg allowlist validate --strict
 ```
 
+**Pruning expired entries:**
+
+```bash
+# Preview expired entries without changing files
+dcg allowlist prune --dry-run
+
+# Remove expired entries from project/user allowlists
+dcg allowlist prune
+```
+
 **Example allowlist.toml:**
 
 ```toml
